soliton.text: Regex character class `_[a-zA-Z-_]*\\.properties` uses `-` in a way that creates an unintended ASCII range (`Z-_`) instead of a literal dash, which can cause incorrect locale-suffix stripping and wrong English file resolution
Locale-suffix stripping regex uses `*` quantifier, allowing an empty suffix (e.g., `_.properties`) to match, which is likely unintended

Path rewriting uses `file.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("resources-community", "resources")`, which can replace unintended occurrences outside the intended directory segment and resolve the wrong English file path
Error handling is inconsistent: `verifySafeHtml()` wraps IO failures in `RuntimeException` while the caller uses `MojoExecutionException`, leading to unhelpful Maven failure reporting/stack traces

`POLICY_SOME_HTML`, `POLICY_NO_HTML`, and `HTML_TAGS` are named like constants but are non-static, non-final, and not private, causing unnecessary per-instance rebuilding and inconsistent style versus existing `private static final` constants
Method name typo `santizeAnchors` should be `sanitizeAnchors` (misspelling at both declaration and call site)

AccessTokenContext constructor mistakenly calls Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null rawTokenId not allowed") instead of validating rawTokenId, allowing null rawTokenId and producing token IDs like "onrtac:null" that collide/break downstream operations

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId test matcher uses wrong substring indices (substring(3,5) instead of (4,6)) and inverts the boolean logic, causing grant-type assertions to pass for wrong shortcuts and fail for correct ones

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider dereferences userSession without a null-check (clientSessionContext.getClientSession().getUserSession() may be null), leading to NPE/HTTP 500 in token issuance paths

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory duplicate-shortcut detection is flawed because ConcurrentHashMap.put overwrites existing entries before size-based collision checks, allowing shortcut collisions to go undetected and later causing decode failures

OAuth2GrantTypeFactory SPI adds getShortcut() as an abstract method without a default implementation, causing binary incompatibility and AbstractMethodError at startup for third-party/out-of-tree implementations

OAuth2GrantType.Context copy constructor (public Context(Context)) was removed from public SPI without replacement, causing NoSuchMethodError/compile failures for external extensions that clone/wrap Context

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider token-id decoding handles legacy tokens gracefully but throws IllegalArgumentException for new tokens with unknown shortcuts, breaking rolling upgrades/heterogeneous clusters by turning unknown prefixes into HTTP 500s
getTokenContextFromTokenId throws on attacker-controlled token IDs and includes the full raw token ID in exception messages, risking HTTP 500s and sensitive log injection/leakage of attacker input

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory lazy shortcut-map refresh updates two ConcurrentHashMaps without synchronization, allowing races that break the bijection invariant and can miss duplicate shortcuts registered post-startup

AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper changed getShouldUseLightweightToken from instance method to public static, silently disabling subclass overrides (static hiding), causing behavioral regressions without compile-time errors
CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential serializes and sends raw plaintext recovery codes to user-storage providers via updateCredential, exposing long-lived bearer secrets in cleartext
RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean calls Optional.get() without checking presence when no recovery credential exists, causing NoSuchElementException and a 500 during login form rendering

CredentialHelper stores a bare JSON List<String> of plaintext codes as credentialData, but RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.createFromCredentialModel expects hashed RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialData JSON, leading to deserialization/type mismatch and authentication failures/NPEs
BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage.isValid validates recovery codes without invalidating/removing the used code, allowing infinite replay of one-time recovery codes
CredentialHelper hard-codes the provider ID string "keycloak-recovery-authn-codes" instead of using RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialProviderFactory.PROVIDER_ID, risking drift and null provider/NPE

CredentialHelper fallback logic misinterprets updateCredential’s boolean return (handled vs not handled), which can cause double-writes, stale federated data taking precedence, or skipping local persistence for non-federated users
RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential prefers federated credentials over local without warning when both exist, potentially shadowing local credentials after provider migration and locking users out

Breaking change to CLI exit-code contract: RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE changed from 4 to 3 and exit code 4 is reused for FEATURE_DISABLED, which can silently break existing automation branching on exit code 4
Missing documentation/deprecation path for the external exit-code contract change (no upgrade guide entry noted)
Test testFeatureNotEnabled only asserts the error message and does not assert the expected exit code, leaving the numeric exit-code contract untested and prone to regression

Custom-image Dockerfile hardcodes --features=rolling-updates into kc.sh build, which bakes a preview feature into derived images by default and may contradict the intended feature gating if the Dockerfile is user-facing/undocumented

ClientRemovedEvent cleanup calls ClientPermissionsV2.setPermissionsEnabled(client, false), but that method unconditionally throws UnsupportedOperationException in V2, causing runtime failure and potentially aborting client deletion transactions

registerListener wraps RoleRemovedEvent/ClientRemovedEvent/GroupRemovedEvent cleanup in Profile.Feature.ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ only, so deployments using other fine-grained-authz flag variants stop running cleanup and accumulate orphan Resource/Policy records after deletions

ClientPermissionsV2.hasPermission(ClientModel, String) fallback path can throw NullPointerException when getResourceTypeResource(...) returns null and the result is passed into policyStore.findByResource(server, resource) without a null guard

ClientPermissionsV2.getClientsWithPermission omits clients that are authorized only via the wildcard 'Clients' resource because it only iterates resources returned by findByType for per-client resources

ClientPermissionsV2.canMapRoles/canMapCompositeRoles/canMapClientScopeRoles lack the MANAGE_CLIENTS admin-role fast-path present in V1, causing admins with only MANAGE_CLIENTS to be denied role-mapping unless explicit MAP_ROLES* policies exist

ClientPermissionsV2.canView(ClientScopeModel)/canManage(ClientScopeModel) reuse the Clients resource type instead of a dedicated ClientScopes resource type, widening access so principals with client view/manage permissions implicitly gain visibility/manage over all client scopes

GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() incorrectly treats having only the VIEW scope on the all-groups resource as sufficient to pass a MANAGE check, enabling read-to-write privilege escalation via the no-arg canManage() path

getGroupIdsWithViewPermission adds groupResource.getId() (authorization Resource UUID) instead of the group model ID (stored as resource name), causing per-group permission checks to miss and silently fall back to type-level all-groups permissions

getGroupIdsWithViewPermission returns authorization Resource UUIDs that are later used where group model IDs are expected (e.g., UsersResource.getUsersCount and session UserModel.GROUPS attribute), breaking group membership filtering and producing incorrect user results (often empty/zero)

UsersResource.searchForUser removes the per-user usersEvaluator.canView post-filter and relies only on an optional groupIds store-layer hint; when groupIds is empty for QUERY_USERS-only callers, the endpoint can return all users, enabling user enumeration
BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser has the same missing canView post-filter pattern as UsersResource.searchForUser, creating the same user-enumeration risk

UserPermissionsV2 canView(UserModel) and canManage(UserModel) no longer include AdminRoles.ADMIN in the role-based fast-path, potentially causing admins with non-standard composites to lose access and receive 403s unless fine-grained permissions are configured

AdminPermissions.registerListener now registers the listener only if the feature flag is enabled at registration time, so enabling the feature after registerListener runs (e.g., in tests or future reload scenarios) would prevent cleanup events from firing and can leave orphaned permission resources in the database

ModelToRepresentation.searchGroupModelsByAttributes was removed despite being a public static helper in server-spi-private, creating a binary-incompatible change for downstream extensions; a deprecated delegate or migration note is needed

Adding abstract method order() to public CryptoProvider SPI is source/binary incompatible and can break third-party implementations (compile failures or AbstractMethodError at runtime); should be a default method for backward compatibility
AuthzClientCryptoProvider.concatenatedRSToASN1DER contains dead allocations: two ASN1Encoder instances write BigIntegers but their results are discarded, misleading and unnecessary
CryptoIntegration.detectProvider behavior changed from fail-fast on multiple providers to silently selecting highest order and only debug-logging ignored providers, which can hide misconfiguration

CryptoIntegration.detectProvider can select non-deterministically when multiple providers have the same order (e.g., both return 200) due to ServiceLoader iteration order; should throw on ties or otherwise handle explicitly

ASN1Decoder.readLength returns -1 for indefinite-length (0x80) and callers pass it to read(length), causing NegativeArraySizeException instead of a clear IOException; DER forbids indefinite length so it should fail fast with IOException

ECDSAAlgorithmTest lacks negative/malformed-input tests for the new hand-rolled ASN.1 parser (e.g., invalid tags, high-tag-form, indefinite/oversized lengths, wrong element count, length mismatches, truncated inputs), leaving security-sensitive failure modes untested
GroupAdapter.getSubGroupsCount() now returns null when modelSupplier.get() returns null, which can trigger NullPointerException in callers that auto-unbox to primitive long or call longValue()
Returning null from GroupModel.getSubGroupsCount() violates/contradicts the interface contract (non-nullable return not documented), forcing unexpected null-guarding on callers
GroupTest.java reader thread lacks a timeout/join, risking hanging/flaky tests

Possible compile error: UsernameForm.authenticate() calls isConditionalPasskeysEnabled() with zero arguments, but only a one-argument overload (isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)) is defined/visible; should likely pass context.getUser() or ensure a zero-arg overload exists in the superclass chain.
Unused helper method `_create_process_for_shard(self, shard: int)` is defined but never called, leaving dead code that may drift from `_create_process_for_shards`
Metric tag key is inconsistent for the same shard dimension (`shard` vs `shards`) across emitted metrics, fragmenting dashboards/alerts
Metric tag value shape is inconsistent for the shard dimension (single-shard integer vs comma-joined multi-shard string), causing inconsistent aggregation/grouping
`SpanFlusher.__init__` accepts a caller-supplied `SpansBuffer` but discards it (only reads `assigned_shards`) and constructs new per-process buffers, silently dropping caller initialization/state
Creating new per-process buffers and pickling copies into child processes multiplies Redis client instances/state (up to ~3x per shard group), increasing resource usage unnecessarily

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result can slice a Django QuerySet with a negative start_offset when cursor.is_prev is true, causing an unconditional AssertionError crash (negative indexing not supported)
cursor.offset is derived from client-supplied ?cursor= and is attacker-controllable, enabling crafted cursors to trigger unhandled 500s/DoS via the negative-slice crash path
Inline comments in paginator.py incorrectly claim Django QuerySets handle negative slicing/boundary conditions automatically (hallucinated behavior)
OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key calls math.floor/math.ceil on a datetime field (from order_by='-datetime'), raising TypeError and crashing pagination/cursor construction
organization_auditlogs.py dereferences organization_context.member.has_global_access without checking member for None, causing AttributeError for non-superusers without active org membership
Authorization gate incorrectly uses has_global_access as a privilege check, allowing ordinary members (e.g., open-membership orgs) to access the advanced/optimized audit-log pagination path
BasePaginator.get_result clamps negative forward-cursor offsets to 0, silently masking cursor corruption/invariant violations instead of failing loudly (should at least log/warn)
Comments in cursors.py/paginator.py falsely claim negative offsets enable efficient reverse/bidirectional pagination; this is incorrect and misleading
OptimizedCursorPaginator docstring falsely advertises streamlined boundary handling/optimized query path despite being a near-copy with crashing branches and misleading claims

Lock narrowing in bleveBackend.BuildIndex introduces a TOCTOU race where concurrent same-key calls can both build an index and the second overwrites/discards the first, causing wasted work and incorrect metrics
Concurrent same-key BuildIndex with file-backed bleve.New can target the same on-disk directory, leading to failures, duplicate handles, orphaned/corrupted index directories
Concurrent same-key BuildIndex with in-memory bleve.NewMemOnly can silently discard the losing index and its written documents, while metrics (IndexMetrics.IndexTenants) are double-incremented
NewResourceServer now runs synchronous index initialization (s.Init(ctx)) under the caller-provided context, so caller deadlines/cancellation can abort startup and make construction fail

Removing per-RPC lazy-init guards means if callers bypass NewResourceServer, ignore its error return, or construct *server directly in tests, initialization may be skipped and failures occur later without the previous safety net

MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but implements none of its required abstract methods (get_dedupe_value, get_group_key_values, build_occurrence_and_event_data), so instantiation raises a TypeError and breaks metric-alert detector evaluation

process_detectors assumes handler.evaluate() returns a dict and calls .values(); any DetectorHandler subclass still returning a list will crash at runtime with AttributeError ('list' object has no attribute values')
Dataclass field `queued` uses `timezone.now()` as a bare default, so it’s evaluated once at import time and shared across instances instead of being set per instance
`to_dict()` emits a raw `datetime` that isn’t JSON-serializable for Celery kwargs, potentially raising a `TypeError` or forcing unintended encoding

`from_dict()` can accept a serialized datetime string and pass it into the `queued: datetime` field without parsing, silently producing an `AssignmentSource` where `queued` is a `str` and violating the type contract

Calling `installation.should_sync("outbound_assignee", parsed_assignment_source)` with a new positional argument can break integration subclasses that still implement the old 1-parameter `should_sync(self, attribute)` signature, causing a `TypeError` at runtime
Uses Python 3.13-only APIs (queue.ShutDown exception and Queue.shutdown()), causing AttributeError on Python <=3.12 and leaving worker threads blocked on work_queue.get() during shutdown
Shutdown path joins daemon worker threads with a timeout while they may still be processing, so the process can tear them down mid-work and drop in-flight items
FixedQueuePool.shutdown() closes/cancels workers without draining already-submitted items, leaving offsets in OffsetTracker.outstanding and preventing the commit loop from advancing
wait_until_empty() exists but is not invoked from SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy.close()/terminate() or ResultsStrategyFactory.shutdown(), so graceful shutdown does not drain queues before stopping
OffsetTracker never evicts per-partition state on Kafka partition revocation, so all_offsets/outstanding/last_committed/partition_locks grow unbounded and leak memory/locks over time
FixedQueuePool.submit() records offsets as added before the work item is safely enqueued; if enqueue/grouping fails, offsets can become committable even though the work was never executed
_commit_loop has a shutdown race where setting shutdown_event can cause the loop to exit without performing a final committable-offset computation/commit, dropping the last bit of progress
OptimizedCursorPaginator uses a negative start_offset for Django QuerySet slicing when cursor.offset < 0, triggering AssertionError and crashing paginated requests
BasePaginator allows negative offset to reach Django QuerySet slicing when cursor.is_prev is true, enabling a trivially reachable DoS across paginated endpoints
Redis Lua script uses ZUNIONSTORE without specifying AGGREGATE MAX, defaulting to SUM and inflating scores for spans present in both sets, corrupting eviction ordering

Cursor.__init__ no longer validates non-negative offsets from attacker-controlled cursor strings, allowing negative offsets to propagate and potentially cause DoS or unintended negative-index behavior on non-QuerySet sequences

organization_auditlogs advanced/optimized pagination authorization gate is too permissive (allows has_global_access members), enabling non-staff users to trigger the advanced pagination path and associated risks

_load_segment_data removed the max_segment_spans guard, eliminating defense-in-depth/telemetry and allowing oversized segments to be loaded without bounds if Lua-side eviction is bypassed or regresses
Git submodule `sentry-repo` is added as a gitlink but `.gitmodules` is not updated/added, so `git submodule update --init` (and CI checkouts) cannot resolve it

`_is_error_focused_query` uses a naive substring check (`"event.type:error" in query.lower()`) that misclassifies negated/compound/quoted queries (e.g., `!event.type:error`, `event.type:error_something`) and can incorrectly rewrite queries to `upsampled_count()`
60-second allowlist eligibility cache can keep upsampling enabled after a project is removed from the allowlist, because `invalidate_upsampling_cache` is not wired to any option-update path

Cache key uses Python `hash(tuple(...))`, which is process-local/unstable for non-int tuple contents and can cause different workers to generate different keys (defeating caching) if `project_ids` ever include non-ints
`_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling` uses `except Exception: pass`, silently swallowing unexpected errors and potentially causing tests to skip setting `sample_rate` without noticing
An unrelated user-facing typo/message fix is mixed into the feature PR, increasing diff noise and complicating reverts/review
`transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling` is redundantly called in multiple branches in `_get_event_stats`, risking future branch divergence and unnecessary duplication

`upsampled_count` (`toInt64(sum(sample_weight))`) assumes `sample_weight` is non-null for all rows; NULL `sample_weight` rows would be skipped by `sum`, causing silent undercounting unless handled (e.g., `ifNull`/`sumIf`) or guaranteed by ingestion/tests

Denial-cache key construction in userPermDenialCacheKey uses '_' as a delimiter even though components may contain '_', causing cache-key collisions between different (action,name,parent) tuples and incorrect denied results
Permission denial cache entries are never invalidated when permissions are granted, so previously denied users can remain blocked until shortCacheTTL expires (stale 403/availability regression)
permissionCacheUsage("false") metric is now emitted for cache-hit-but-denied cases as well as true cache misses, changing metric semantics and inflating the 'false' counter

In Check, non-cache-miss errors from getCachedIdentityPermissions (e.g., GetUserIdentifiers failures or unsupported identity type) are treated as cache misses and swallowed, causing duplicate failing lookups and hiding the underlying error/incorrect metric attribution
Uncaught HTTPError (e.g., 401 Unauthorized) from get_user_info() propagates and crashes the OAuth install flow with a 500 instead of rendering a graceful error page
Direct key access integration.metadata["sender"]["login"] can raise KeyError when metadata is missing/differently shaped, causing a 500 (and potential info disclosure) instead of failing closed
Race condition: OAuth callback may arrive before the installation webhook persists integration.metadata["sender"]["login"], causing legitimate installs to fail (invalid request or KeyError)
Installer identity is derived from webhook-persisted metadata instead of querying GitHub (e.g., /app/installations/{id}), making the authorization check unreliable under race/jitter
Removing FORWARD_INSTALL_FOR constant from pipeline_advancer.py can break external importers/tests/plugins via ImportError
OAuth state uses pipeline.signature (not a fresh per-request nonce), allowing replay within the pipeline window and weakening CSRF protection
OAuth state comparison uses plain != instead of constant-time comparison (e.g., hmac.compare_digest), enabling potential timing side-channel leakage
Test test_github_user_mismatch mutates webhook payload but uses a hardcoded HMAC signature that won’t match the mutated body, causing webhook signature validation to fail and the test to break

installation_id can be overwritten via request.GET on the OAuth callback leg, enabling mid-flow installation swap and incorrect identity binding; should bind once and treat bound state as authoritative
Bare except Exception during OAuth token exchange parsing silently swallows real failures (network/parse/programming errors) without logging, hindering debugging and incident investigation
Test cleanup registers literal "alias" instead of the loop variable when adding cleanup callbacks, so created IDPs are never removed and tests can become flaky due to leaked realm state
The later creation of "idp-alias-20" also lacks correct cleanup registration (should remove "idp-alias-20"), further leaking IDPs across test runs

In getForLogin(), the cache-hit path returns raw IdentityProviderModel instances while cache-miss/fallback paths return createOrganizationAwareIdentityProviderModel-wrapped instances, causing inconsistent behavior between cache hits and misses

Login cache invalidation is not triggered when IDP↔organization membership changes via OrganizationProvider addIdentityProvider/removeIdentityProvider (without going through IdentityProviderStorageProvider.update), leaving stale cached login IDP sets
getLoginPredicate() adds a clause that dereferences idp.getConfig() without a null check, risking a NullPointerException if getConfig() can be null
getLoginPredicate() must remain aligned with JpaIdentityProviderStorageProvider.getForLogin() DB filtering; any mismatch can make cache invalidation unsound and allow stale entries to persist
Test scenario 4 conflates update() and addIdentityProvider(), so it cannot verify that addIdentityProvider alone triggers cache invalidation
Tests do not cover cache invalidation when a login-eligible IDP is removed (they only remove a disabled/non-eligible IDP), so regressions in remove-path invalidation could go undetected
enableSqlExpressions always returns false because both branches return false, making the feature-flag lookup have no effect

enableSqlExpressions assigns enabled as the negation of the flag state, so a future simplification (e.g., returning enabled) would invert intended behavior and could accidentally re-enable the CVE path
No comment documents that SQL expressions are intentionally always disabled as a mitigation for CVE-2024-9264, making the security intent easy to miss
No regression test pins the contract that SQL expressions must remain disabled even if FlagSqlExpressions is set, so accidental re-enablement could slip through CI

pkg/expr/sql/parser.go preserves a vulnerable fmt.Sprintf-based SQL string interpolation pattern with naive quote escaping, which is unsafe for DuckDB and could reintroduce CVE-2024-9264 if the stub is replaced with a real engine
TablesList currently builds and would execute the vulnerable SQL command shape instead of early-returning an explicit disabled error, leaving a dormant security gadget in the codebase
Error messages like "sqlExpressions is not implemented" / "not implemented" are misleading because the feature is intentionally disabled for CVE-2024-9264, lacking actionable context for operators
ReadQuery rejects SQL expressions without logging/metrics, so existing rules may fail silently and operators lack visibility into attempted SQL expression usage

Double-checked locking is incomplete: after acquiring the write lock, the code does not re-check whether another goroutine has already populated entryPointAssetsCache, causing redundant asset loads during cold-start contention
On error, the function unconditionally assigns entryPointAssetsCache = result even when result is nil, which can overwrite and clear a previously valid cache entry after a transient load failure
Cleanup ticker interval changed from 10 minutes to 1 minute in pkg/services/cleanup/cleanup.go:77, likely an unintended debug leftover and increases lock contention risk
Six routine cleanup progress logs are emitted at Error level on the happy path in pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:239, polluting error logs and triggering false alerts
A happy-path log statement includes the field "err" even though err is guaranteed nil at that point, making the log misleading
Happy-path logs include full ID slices ("ids", ids), which can cause log flooding and increased ingestion costs on large tables

SQLite >999-parameter branch builds the IN(...) list via repeated fmt.Sprintf string concatenation in a loop (O(n^2)) in pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:323; should use strings.Builder or similar
Cleanup interval is hardcoded (and potentially shortened) in pkg/services/cleanup/cleanup.go:77, removing an operator tuning knob; should be configurable (e.g., via grafana.ini)
fetchIDs accepts a raw WHERE fragment, creating a fragile API surface prone to misuse
Renaming TestAnnotationCleanUp to TestIntegrationAnnotationCleanUp may cause it to be skipped by CI -run filters that reference the old name
Async legacy write goroutines derive their context from the cancellable request context, so the context is canceled when the handler returns and legacy writes are silently dropped
Create: when d.Storage.Create returns an error, the code records the failure using recordLegacyDuration instead of recordStorageDuration, mis-attributing the error in metrics
Update: on storage failure, the code records the failure using recordLegacyDuration instead of recordStorageDuration, mis-attributing the error in metrics
Delete: recordStorageDuration is called with the object name passed as the kind label, creating unbounded Prometheus label cardinality
DeleteCollection: the async legacy goroutine records duration via recordStorageDuration instead of recordLegacyDuration, double-counting storage and missing legacy metrics
Update: legacy update no longer uses an updateWrapper to force legacy to persist the storage-updated object, so legacy recomputes a potentially different object and drifts from storage
Delete: ctx is set with klog.NewContext(ctx, d.Log) instead of the enriched per-call logger, so name/kind/method fields are lost from context logging
Async legacy goroutines lack panic recovery, so a panic inside the goroutine can crash the apiserver process
Tests: Mode 3 tests reference a bare package-scope prometheus registry variable p whose declaration may be missing, potentially breaking compilation
Tests: sharing a mutable package-level prometheus.Registry across tests/subtests can cause duplicate collector registration panics (especially with parallel tests)

Federated rule detection was removed in useAllRulerRuleAbilities (isFederated hardcoded to false), causing federated Mimir rule groups to be treated as mutable and enabling Edit/Delete/Pause actions that will fail against the backend

React key prop was dropped for Grafana rows in FilterView when replacing GrafanaRuleLoader with GrafanaRuleListItem, leading to missing unique keys, React warnings, and unstable per-row state across re-renders
Grafana rule detail links no longer include the returnTo query parameter in GrafanaRuleListItem, breaking navigation context so users can’t return to the exact filtered list/scroll position
TOCTOU race in CreateOrUpdateDevice: CountDevices check and subsequent INSERT/UPSERT are separate SQL statements without transaction/locking, allowing concurrent requests to exceed device_limit

Authenticate now calls TagDevice synchronously on the request context, so slow/stalled DB operations can block anonymous authentication indefinitely (or until DB timeout), increasing latency and enabling DoS amplification
Using the request context for TagDevice means client disconnect cancels ctx mid INSERT/UPDATE, potentially leaving inconsistent writes and generating cancellation errors in logs
Removal of panic-recovery around TagDevice means a panic in the tagging path can crash the auth handler

updateDevice returns ErrDeviceLimitReached when rowsAffected == 0, conflating device-not-found or stale-window update misses with actual device-limit enforcement, breaking error semantics for Authenticate/logging
anonymousDeviceExpiration constant is duplicated in anonstore and api packages, creating two sources of truth that can drift and cause inconsistent expiration/listing behavior
When device_limit > 0, CreateOrUpdateDevice performs an extra COUNT(*) round-trip on every anonymous request, adding high-RPS DB load on potentially large tables

Frontend anonymousDeviceLimit type/initialization (number | undefined, default undefined) conflicts with backend always-sent numeric field (0 when disabled), causing incorrect feature gating based on undefined checks

I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded! will raise NoMethodError if any environment file still sets config.i18n.fallbacks = true, because Rails after_initialize overwrites I18n.fallbacks with I18n::Locale::Fallbacks (which lacks ensure_loaded!)
Background jobs/rake tasks set I18n.locale without calling I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!, so fallback locales are never loaded outside web requests and translations won’t fall back as expected
FallbackLocaleList does not implement I18n::Locale::Fallbacks#defaults, so I18n::Backend::Fallbacks calling I18n.fallbacks.defaults may raise NoMethodError during translation fallback resolution
FallbackLocaleList#[] calls SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym unconditionally, which will crash with NoMethodError when default_locale is nil (e.g., during boot/tests/settings unavailable)

`traceID` is no longer included in the "Plugin Request Completed" log line because the new logging path relies on `FromContext` and `instrumentContext` does not attach `traceID`, potentially breaking trace correlation for plugin request logs

`instrumentContext` in `contextual_logger_middleware.go` only attaches `endpoint`, `pluginId`, `dsName`, `dsUID`, and `uname` and omits `traceID`, so downstream logs may lose correlation unless infra logging adds it independently

`grafanaInfraLogWrapper.FromContext` silently falls back to `d.New()` when the underlying logger type assertion fails, wiping all contextual attributes with no warning/metric, causing observability regressions if the infra logger type changes

`TestLogger.FromContext` in `pkg/plugins/log/fake.go` returns a new `NewTestLogger()` instance, so logs emitted via `FromContext` won't be captured in the original test logger's `InfoLogs`/`ErrorLogs`, making middleware tests unreliable

No tests were added for `ContextualLoggerMiddleware`, so nothing verifies that its endpoint wrappers call `instrumentContext` and attach `pluginId`/`endpoint`/`dsName`/`dsUID`/`uname` to context before forwarding requests

Test/file naming is inconsistent after the rename: `instrumentation_middleware_test.go` was renamed but the top-level test function is still `TestInstrumentationMiddleware`, which is stale relative to `MetricsMiddleware`
SQL injection risk in migration db/migrate/20150818190757_create_embeddable_hosts.rb:18 due to interpolating admin-writable site_settings.value directly into raw INSERT SQL
Migration can break on non-malicious host values containing single quotes because raw SQL string interpolation is not escaped
embeddable_hosts_controller uses EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first and then calls methods on nil in update/destroy, causing NoMethodError/500 instead of 404 on invalid IDs
embeddable_hosts_controller save_host reads params[:embeddable_host][:host] without strong parameters and without guarding missing parent key, risking exceptions and unsafe mass assignment patterns
embeddable_hosts_controller authorization may be insufficient because ensure_staff might allow non-admins to modify the embed allowlist
EmbeddableHost model before_validation calls host.sub! unconditionally, raising NoMethodError when host is nil instead of producing a validation error
Frontend store.js.es6 hydration branch calls self.pluralize(subType) but pluralize is undefined on the store object, causing runtime TypeError and breaking all _ids hydration
store.js.es6 calls obj[k].map(...) without guarding for null/undefined, causing TypeError if the server returns an _ids field as null
Migration uses create_table :embeddable_hosts, force: true which can drop existing embeddable_hosts data on migration re-run/redo

Fabricator definitions are in the wrong files: category_fabricator.rb contains :embeddable_host and embeddable_host_fabricator.rb contains category fabricators, creating a maintainability trap and potential duplicate-fabricator load errors
EmbeddableHost.record_for_host does not normalize hostname case before querying lower(host), causing legitimate mixed/upper-case hosts to fail allowlist matching
EmbeddableHost host validation regex incorrectly limits TLD length to 5 characters, rejecting valid modern TLDs
EmbeddableHost host validation regex permits path suffixes (/(\/.*)?/), allowing stored host values with paths and increasing risk if reflected unsanitized
EmbeddableHost host validation regex does not reject '@' userinfo components in URLs/hosts

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291, the light-theme value for `.reply-count a` was incorrectly changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%)`, unintentionally lightening the link color in light mode

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291, the dark-theme arm for `.reply-count a` uses `scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%)` but should be the inverted value `70%` (100-30) to match the intended dark-theme mapping

In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/modal.scss:102, the `dark-light-choose` arguments for `.custom-message-length` are inverted, causing the light-theme color to use `$primary` at 30% instead of preserving the original 70%

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/user.scss:523, the light-theme value for `.name` was incorrectly changed from `$primary` at 30% to 50%, removing the intended visual distinction from the `.title` element

In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/topic-post.scss:183, the light-theme value for the `h3` heading was incorrectly changed from `$primary` at 20% to 50%, significantly lightening the heading in light mode
In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/user.scss:498, the light-theme value for `.name` was incorrectly changed from `$primary` at 30% to 50%, making it visually identical to `.title` in light mode
GroupsController#members uses an unbounded `limit` param, allowing extremely large requests that can cause memory/serialization DoS

Admin GroupsController add_members/remove_member mutate GroupUser associations before `group.save`, so a failed save can return an error while membership changes have already been persisted (non-transactional write)
admin-group.js totalPages calculation is off-by-one when user_count is an exact multiple of limit, causing an extra empty page and incorrect pagination UI
Group model findMembers returns undefined when name is empty (`return;`) instead of a resolved promise/array, breaking callers that chain `.then(...)`
Group create/update controller actions changed request param shape (top-level params instead of `params[:group]`) without versioning/deprecation, risking breaking external API consumers
GroupsController#members response shape changed from a bare array to `{members: [...], meta: {...}}` without versioning/deprecation, risking breaking external API consumers
Admin route `/admin/groups/:id/users` was removed; plugins/themes or other callers may still depend on it and will 404 without a deprecation path or compatibility alias

Missing tests for new/changed behaviors: non-automatic group update via top-level params, add_members with nonexistent usernames, remove_member when user isn’t a member, and pagination meta correctness across pages
Stray semicolon/ASI-dependent early return in group.js (`if (!this.get('id')) { return };`) is stylistically inconsistent and should be `return;`
Non-atomic read-modify-write of BlockedEmail.match_count in should_block? can lose increments under concurrent requests
should_block? ignores the return value of record.save, potentially swallowing persistence failures

should_block? is called from EmailValidator#validate_each and can run multiple times per registration (multiple valid? calls), causing match_count to be incremented more than once per attempt and breaking counter semantics
email_in_restriction_setting? builds a regex from SiteSetting with insufficient escaping (only '.'), allowing regex metacharacters through and enabling ReDoS via catastrophic backtracking
email_in_restriction_setting? can raise RegexpError on invalid patterns (e.g., '*.example.com'), potentially breaking signup sitewide (DoS)
create_account_controller.js pushes result.values.email without checking it exists, allowing undefined into rejectedEmails and causing spurious invalid state when accountEmail is undefined/empty
User profile template binds to `model.website_name` but Discourse JS models camelize serializer fields to `websiteName`, so the website name will render as empty/undefined

Ruby serializer uses mutating concatenation (`"." << website_host`) on a string literal, which can raise `FrozenError` under `# frozen_string_literal: true` and is semantically misleading for simple concatenation
`URI(website.to_s)` is parsed multiple times per call and only the first parse is rescue-guarded; later `.path` accesses are unguarded and can raise on malformed URIs while also being inefficient

Docstring annotation in `user.js.es6` was changed from `@property websiteName` to `@property profileBackground`, potentially removing/misleading documentation for an existing `websiteName` computed property and obscuring whether the model already provides it
Using Ruby Kernel#open/open-uri on untrusted URLs enables SSRF and potential command execution when URL begins with '|'
Anonymous visitors can influence @embed_url reaching TopicEmbed.import_remote, allowing arbitrary host fetches via the embed pipeline
New raw_html cook_method bypasses PrettyText/Sanitize and returns post body verbatim, enabling stored XSS from imported RSS/remote content
Rendering cooked content with raw in best.html.erb executes unsanitized HTML/JS in the Discourse origin
embed.html.erb uses request.referer as postMessage targetOrigin, but targetOrigin must be an origin (scheme://host[:port]) not a full URL, breaking the handshake
request.referer is interpolated into a single-quoted JS string without escaping, allowing JS injection via a crafted Referer header
Parent-side postMessage origin validation uses indexOf substring matching instead of exact origin equality, allowing spoofed origins to pass
Embed access control relies on Referer host comparison, which is suppressible/spoofable and not a reliable authorization mechanism
Setting X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL is invalid/unsupported, so framing protection/allowance is unreliable; should use CSP frame-ancestors instead
poll_feed.rb calls i.content.scrub when RSS items may lack <content>, causing NoMethodError and aborting the scheduled job
Redis throttle uses SETNX then EXPIRE in separate commands, risking a permanent key without TTL if a crash/failover occurs between commands
Migration uses force: true on create_table, which can drop existing data when migrations are replayed/reset
Concurrent imports of the same embed_url can race and raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique, causing a 500 and potentially leaving an orphan topic
app/views/embed/best.html.erb contains '<%- end if %>' which is a Ruby syntax error and prevents the template from rendering
Duplicate OptimizedImage.downsize method definitions cause Ruby to silently override the legacy 4-argument signature, breaking existing callers and potentially crashing when opts is not a Hash
Server-side remote URL download limit is hardcoded to 10MB, bypassing admin-configured SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb and enabling larger-than-policy downloads
Client-side upload size checks and 413 messaging are hardcoded to 10MB, ignoring admin max_<type>_size_kb/max_image_size_kb settings and ignoring the upload type
OptimizedImage.downsize now accepts an unvalidated dimensions string that flows into shell-executed ImageMagick commands, widening shell-injection/RCE risk if any caller passes untrusted input
Upload downsize loop lacks a convergence/no-progress guard and does not check convert success, so it may exhaust attempts without shrinking and still proceed with an oversized file
Downsizing in-place by using the same tempfile.path for source and destination risks file corruption/truncation and stale size checks; should write to a separate temp file and replace atomically
Invoking ImageMagick up to 5 times per upload increases resource-exhaustion and ImageTragick attack surface; should add safety checks (MIME/pixel limits) and enforce ImageMagick policy restrictions
Unsubscribe action uses TopicUser.find_by which can return nil, causing NoMethodError when accessing tu.notification_level and calling tu.save!
Unsubscribe endpoint is a GET route that mutates server state, making it vulnerable to CSRF and accidental triggering by link previewers/URL scanners
Email locale string requires %{unsubscribe_url} but many mailers/builders won’t pass unsubscribe_url, causing I18n::MissingInterpolationArgument and potentially dropping emails
dropdown-button component concatenates unescaped title into HTML, enabling stored XSS via user-controlled topic titles
unsubscribe.hbs uses triple-brace {{{stopNotificiationsText}}} to render an I18n string that interpolates an unescaped topic title, enabling XSS
perform_show_response now forces render :show, coupling the helper to the show template and breaking future callers that expect their own templates

Unsubscribe action lacks topic existence and authorization checks (no NotFound guard and no guardian.ensure_can_see!), enabling private-topic existence probing and undefined behavior for invalid topic_id
SelectedCalendarRepository.updateManyByCredentialId is called with empty data {}, making updateMany a no-op so Prisma @updatedAt does not bump and the cache-last-updated indicator never refreshes
scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh uses BSD/macOS sed in-place syntax (sed -i '' -E), which fails on GNU sed/Linux and prevents rewriting the .env file

connectedCalendars.handler.ts instantiates CalendarCacheRepository directly, bypassing the feature-flagged CalendarCacheRepositoryMock and causing DB access (and possible runtime errors) when the calendar-cache feature is disabled

deleteCache.handler.ts authorization only checks credentialId + userId and does not account for team-owned or delegation credentials, enabling IDOR/broken access control where users can delete cache for shared/delegation credentials
connectedCalendars.handler.ts uses `|| null` on cacheStatusMap.get(...), which can incorrectly coerce falsy values; should use nullish coalescing (??) for correctness/future-proofing
Working-hours boundary check computes both start and end from slotStartTime, never using slotEndTime, so slots that extend past workingHour.endTime can be incorrectly marked available
Day-off override check compares two Dayjs instances with === (reference equality), making the branch dead and causing full-day unavailability overrides to be ignored
utcOffset is multiplied by -1, inverting the timezone shift direction and causing date-override day matching to use the wrong local date
Potential DST bug: UTC offset sampled only at override.start is reused for override.end, which can be off by ±60 minutes across DST transitions
deleteMany cleanup predicate uses `retryCount > 1` without filtering `method: SMS`, risking deletion of EMAIL/WHATSAPP reminders from the shared `WorkflowReminder` table

If Twilio send succeeds but the subsequent Prisma update fails, the catch path increments `retryCount` without marking the reminder scheduled or saving the SID, causing the SMS to be resent (duplicate send/charge) on the next cron tick

The `await prisma.workflowReminder.update(...)` inside the catch block is unguarded; if it throws, it propagates out and aborts the remaining cron loop iterations and may prevent sending the HTTP response
`retryCount` is updated via non-atomic read-modify-write (`reminder.retryCount + 1`), which can lose increments under concurrent cron runs; should use Prisma atomic `increment`

Retry exhaustion threshold `retryCount > 1` is an unclear/off-by-one magic number; intent (total attempts vs retries) is ambiguous and should use a clearer predicate/constant (e.g., `gte: MAX_SMS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS`)

`retryCount` column is global on `WorkflowReminder` but only SMS handler uses it; sibling email/WhatsApp schedulers don’t increment/clean up, making semantics unclear and increasing risk of divergence or misuse (consider rename/comment or extend pattern)
Exhausted reminders are silently deleted with no logging/metrics, reducing observability into failed-to-deliver SMS
Logging raw Twilio error objects via `console.log` may leak sensitive data (recipient phone numbers/message bodies) into logs

Public InsightsBookingService.findMany() method was removed and replaced with getBaseConditions() without documenting/updating all external call sites, risking compile-time breaks and forcing callers to reimplement where-composition safely
Constructor public options type allows scope:"team" without teamId, but the internal zod discriminated union requires teamId, causing runtime ZodError for calls that compile
Caching test suite (describe("Caching" ...)) was deleted while caching fields/guards remain in production code, reducing coverage and risking silent memoization regressions

Tests assert expect(conditions).toEqual(Prisma.sql`...`) against freshly-built Prisma.Sql fragments, which is fragile because it relies on undocumented internal structure that may change across Prisma versions
Webhook handler trusts body-supplied userId, allowing any caller with the webhook secret to overwrite any user’s stored OAuth credentials (IDOR/broken access control)
symmetricDecrypt uses process.env.CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY || "", so an unset encryption key falls back to empty string and enables trivial/known-key decryption
Webhook secret check can be bypassed when CALCOM_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset because undefined header compared to undefined env var skips the 403 branch (unauthenticated requests accepted)
Webhook secret is compared with a non-constant-time string comparison, enabling timing attacks to recover the secret
parseRefreshTokenResponse overwrites missing refresh_token with the literal string "refresh_token", poisoning stored credentials when providers omit refresh_token on refresh

refreshOAuthTokens sync branch returns a raw fetch Response (not parsed JSON and no status/content-type checks), causing parseRefreshTokenResponse to fail or persist invalid data due to incompatible return types
Webhook endpoint lacks an HTTP method guard, so non-POST verbs can reach decrypt/upsert logic and may leak secrets via logs/caches
symmetricDecrypt and JSON.parse are unguarded; malformed ciphertext/non-JSON causes distinct 500s vs 403/400, creating an oracle and enabling DoS via crashes
Credential-writing webhook has no rate limiting, making timing attacks and request-flood DoS practical
Credential-writing webhook has no strict body-size cap, allowing oversized payloads to amplify decrypt/parse/DB work (DoS risk)
CALCOM_CREDENTIAL_SYNC_ENDPOINT is fetched without protocol/host validation, creating SSRF/misconfiguration risk and allowing attacker-controlled token JSON to be persisted
When credential sync is enabled but userId is null, refreshOAuthTokens silently falls back to direct refresh with no logging, hiding sync bypass/failures
minimumTokenResponseSchema omits refresh_token but code mutates parsed.data.refresh_token, making the returned type inaccurate/unsafe and potentially hiding refresh_token handling bugs
`deleteScheduledEmailReminder` with `immediateDelete=true` cancels in SendGrid but does not delete/update the corresponding `WorkflowReminder` DB row, leaving orphaned/stale reminders on reschedule
In the cron cancellation loop, a single failing `client.request` aborts processing of the remaining reminders because the try/catch wraps the whole loop, starving later items in the batch

When the cron loop aborts early, the trailing `Promise.all(workflowRemindersToDelete)` is skipped, so reminders already cancelled in SendGrid may remain marked `cancelled=true` in DB and be retried indefinitely

Reminder-cancellation promises are dropped in `handleCancelBooking.ts` due to using synchronous `.forEach` without awaiting `deleteScheduled*Reminder`, so the handler can finish before DB writes complete
Dropped/ignored cancellation promises can lead to unhandled promise rejections if helper calls reject (no caller-level await/handling)

The same fire-and-forget reminder-cancellation pattern (missing `await Promise.all`) is duplicated in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx` and multiple call sites in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/workflows.tsx`
In `handleNewBooking.ts`, `try { forEach(async ...) } catch` cannot catch async rejections because `.forEach` doesn’t await, making the outer catch ineffective and allowing errors to escape
In `handleNewBooking.ts`, the outer try/catch is effectively dead code because the helpers already swallow errors internally, so failures may be silently ignored

The new cron only processes `cancelled=true` reminders and the unscheduled pass handles `scheduled=false`, leaving no cleanup path for `scheduled=true` reminders whose `referenceId` is invalid/cancelled (stale rows persist)
Email vs SMS cancellation behavior is inconsistent: SMS deletion path removes the DB row while email `immediateDelete` path does not, creating asymmetry and potential data inconsistency
SendGrid `scheduled_sends` cancel entries may still accumulate because no `DELETE /v3/user/scheduled_sends/{batch_id}` cleanup is performed after cancellation
Unexplained removal of the `|| isSenderIdNeeded` branch in `WorkflowStepContainer.tsx` may change behavior unexpectedly
The new 15-minute cron scan has concerns (details not provided in the comment)
Permission check incorrectly uses logical AND between isTeamAdmin and isTeamOwner, making the team admin/owner authorization branch unreachable
Guest email blacklist check compares lowercased blacklist entries against raw user input, allowing case-variant emails to bypass the blacklist
Attendee deduplication compares emails case-sensitively, allowing duplicate attendee rows when casing differs
Email fan-out passes raw guests instead of filtered uniqueGuests, causing emails to be sent to already-existing/filtered recipients and sending the wrong template (scheduled invite)
AddGuests input schema has no maximum guest count (and no rate limiting), enabling email-bombing/resource exhaustion via large guest arrays
Email send failures are caught and only console.logged without the error and then swallowed, returning success even when notifications fail
Organizer email subject uses calEvent.attendees[0].name, which may be empty because newly added guests have name set to "", producing blank names in the subject
Authorization allows any attendee to add guests without organizer consent, enabling uncontrolled guest additions and leakage of event/video-call details to third parties
Backup codes are stored using reversible symmetric encryption instead of being hashed (e.g., bcrypt/argon2/scrypt), allowing plaintext recovery if the encryption key and DB are compromised
Backup-code consumption is not atomic (TOCTOU): concurrent login requests can reuse the same backup code because read-check-update-write is not done as a single conditional/transactional operation
Backup-code verification uses non-constant-time comparison via Array.indexOf, leaking timing information about match position/prefix and enabling side-channel guessing
React state for backupCodes is initialized with useState([]), inferring never[] and losing type safety for sensitive string values
resetState() does not clear sensitive React state (backupCodes, backupCodesUrl, dataUri, secret), leaving plaintext credentials in memory after closing/canceling the modal
Blob URL created for downloading backup codes is not revoked on close/unmount, causing a memory leak and leaving plaintext backup codes URL-accessible for the document lifetime
Server-side backupCode input lacks sufficient length/format validation beyond stripping dashes
E2E tests do not cover backup-code verification paths (login and disabling 2FA with backup codes), leaving regression gaps; existing test includes TODO/FIXME indicating it may pass incorrectly
EventManager.ts: unguarded access to mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration can throw when evt.destinationCalendar is [] or null, causing a runtime crash for Meet bookings

GoogleCalendarService CalendarService.ts updateEvent: fallback selectedCalendar logic uses a tautological find predicate comparing cal.externalId to a falsy externalCalendarId, so selectedCalendar becomes undefined and calendar.events.update may run with calendarId undefined

GoogleCalendarService CalendarService.ts deleteEvent: same tautological find predicate as updateEvent, so calendarId becomes undefined and calendar.events.delete may target the wrong calendar or fail; non-primary events may never be deleted

LarkCalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection for multi-host events

Office365CalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection for multi-host events
BaseCalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and breaking subclasses (e.g., CalDAV/Apple) that inherit it

handleNewBooking.ts: initializing evt.destinationCalendar to null causes the COLLECTIVE branch's evt.destinationCalendar?.push(...) to no-op, silently dropping team destination calendars when organizer has no personal destinationCalendar

EventManager.ts updateAllCalendarEvents: updateEvent can be invoked with an undefined credential after DB fallback fails to populate credential (e.g., app uninstalled), causing CalendarManager.updateEvent to no-op
handleCancelBooking.ts: credential DB refetch omits app.slug include/mapping, so calendarCredential.appName becomes undefined and downstream logs/emails/metrics keyed on appName break
handleNewBooking.ts loadUsers dynamic-user branch: organization.slug is no longer selected, so downstream user.organization?.slug becomes undefined for dynamic bookings in org context
handleNewBooking.ts loadUsers host branch: function can return undefined instead of [], leading to crashes in callers that assume an array (e.g., users.slice / users.push)

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in tRPC reschedule path (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx:553) are not awaited, so calendar.deleteEvent runs after the mutation resolves and errors become unhandled rejections

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in vital reschedule (packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts:125) are not awaited, so deletions run fire-and-forget and errors bypass the enclosing try/catch

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in wipemycalother reschedule (packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts:125) are not awaited, so deletions are not awaited and errors become unhandled rejections

Recurring-event deletion loop in handleCancelBooking (packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:460) uses forEach(async ...) so recurring deletions are orphaned (not included in Promise.all) and their failures are swallowed

getCalendarCredentials in packages/core/CalendarManager.ts:28 assigns calendar = getCalendar(credential) without awaiting, so the returned calendar field is a Promise<Calendar|null> that can be mistakenly treated as Calendar|null by consumers (truthy checks/method calls)

Dynamic import() calls stored directly in the app-store barrel object (packages/app-store/index.ts:1) execute eagerly at module initialization, defeating intended lazy loading and causing all app modules to start loading immediately

getVideoAdapters in packages/core/videoClient.ts:21 now awaits appStore imports sequentially in a for...of loop, reducing parallelism and adding per-credential module-load latency compared to the prior parallel map/reduce behavior
`fetch_error_details` pairs `error_ids` with `events.values()` from `nodestore.backend.get_multi`, incorrectly relying on dict value ordering; can associate wrong event data with an error
Test `test_get_with_error` only covers a single error, so it cannot detect the potential mis-association bug in `fetch_error_details`
Environment filter (`environment: selection.environments`) was removed from the `/trace-items/attributes/` query; may broaden results across environments if backend previously narrowed by environment

Inline comment claims environment is intentionally omitted because endpoint doesn't support it, but backend behavior is unconfirmed; needs verification and documentation/release note or forward-compatible inclusion
Detector `owner` field is set from request without explicit same-organization validation; potential cross-org actor reference/IDOR risk if `ActorField` isn’t scoped to org
`schedule_webhook_delivery` uses a 30s `processing_deadline_duration`, which may be too short under load for DB scan/enqueue fan-out and could cause timeouts/pages
Default project option `sentry:autofix_automation_tuning` changed from `"low"` to `"off"` without migration/release note/flag; silently disables autofix for projects that didn’t set it
`feedback_summaries.make_input_prompt` changed formatting from bulleted list to `------` delimiter, altering LLM prompt shape and potentially impacting output quality/evals
Using `------` as a delimiter can collide with user-provided content containing the same string, causing adjacent feedback entries to merge; delimiter should be escaped or made collision-resistant

Migrations `0917` and `0920` were changed to early `return` and reference `0921_convert_org_saved_searches_to_views_rerevised.py`, which is not included; migration chain may be broken if `0921` isn’t already on main
